Class CombinationGeneratorTask

java.lang.Object
java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask<Void>
java.util.concurrent.RecursiveAction
com.github.mrgarbagegamer.CombinationGeneratorTask
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Future<Void>
public class CombinationGeneratorTask extends RecursiveAction
A RecursiveAction that generates combinations of clicks for the Lights Out puzzle solver.

This class is the "producer" in a producer-consumer pattern, using a ForkJoinPool to recursively explore the solution space. After a recent refactor, it no longer generates individual combinations. Instead, it creates compact WorkBatch.WorkItem objects that describe a *range* of combinations (a shared prefix and a set of final clicks). These work items are added to a WorkBatch and passed to a CombinationQueue for consumption by "monkeys".

Execution Model

The generation process is a tree-based, divide-and-conquer algorithm. Each task represents a prefix of clicks. The root task forks subtasks for each possible first click, and these subtasks recursively fork children until the desired combination length is reached. To optimize performance, this class implements two key strategies:

  • Constraint Pruning: At each branching point, canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int) uses bitmasks to check if a path can possibly lead to a valid solution, pruning entire branches early.
  • Range-Based Batching: Leaf tasks now define a range of work with a single WorkBatch.addWork(short[], int, boolean, int) call, offloading the final combination enumeration to the monkeys. This significantly reduces CPU load on the generator threads.

Resource Management

To avoid performance degradation from excessive garbage collection, this class adheres to a strict "don't allocate" policy in its hot paths. All critical resources, including short[] prefix arrays and the tasks themselves, are recycled using thread-local pools managed by the GeneratorContext. This design reduces heap allocations to nearly zero during the main generation loop.

Since:
2025.06 - Fork Join Refactor
See Also:
  • ForkJoinTask
  • Serialized Form
Performance Characteristics:
The theoretical complexity is O(C(Grid.NUM_CELLS, numClicks)). However, aggressive bitmask-based pruning and parallel execution significantly reduce the practical workload.
Threading Model:
Tasks are isolated by the ForkJoinTask framework. Shared resources are managed via a ThreadLocal GeneratorContext to ensure thread safety and eliminate contention.
Algorithm Details:
A recursive, divide-and-conquer approach. Tasks form a generation tree where each node is a click prefix. Subtasks are forked until a target length is reached. Leaf tasks generate final combinations, which are batched and queued for validation.
Memory Management:
Object allocations are minimized through extensive use of ArrayPool and TaskPool, managed by a thread-local GeneratorContext.

    Initializes the static TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS and SUFFIX_OR_MASKS for a given puzzle.

    Flushes any remaining pending batches from a single thread's CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext to the CombinationQueueArray.

    Flushes a WorkBatch to the CombinationQueueArray, blocking if necessary until successful.

    Flushes a batch of combinations to an available queue, employing a backoff strategy if all queues are temporarily full.

  • Field Details

    • POOL_SIZE

      private static final int POOL_SIZE
      The pre-allocated size of the thread-local resource pools in CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.

      To avoid heap allocations in the hot path, this class relies on object pooling for both prefix arrays (via ArrayPool) and CombinationGeneratorTask objects (via TaskPool). This constant defines the capacity of those pools.

      Performance Considerations

      The pool size balances memory footprint against pool misses.

      • Larger pools: Reduce the chance of a pool miss (which would force a new allocation) at the cost of higher upfront memory usage.
      • Smaller pools: Conserve memory but risk contention or misses if the task recursion depth exceeds the pool capacity.
      The value 512 was chosen as a safe capacity that prevents pool misses under typical conditions without excessive memory overhead.

      Since:
      2025.06 - Array Pooling Introduction
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as a static final constant.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • logger

      private static final Logger logger
      The logger for this class.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Final Flush Refactor
      See Also:
      • flushAllPendingBatches()
      • Logger.debug(String, Object, Object)
      • Logger.info(String)
      • Logger.info(String, Object)
      • LogManager.getLogger()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to the design of Log4j2.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~4 bytes as a reference.

    • context

      private static final ThreadLocal<CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext> context
      A ThreadLocal container for the CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.

      This provides each generator thread with its own isolated set of resources (array pools, task pools, and the current WorkBatch). Using a single ThreadLocal to hold a context object is a key optimization that avoids the high cost of multiple ThreadLocal.get() calls in the hot path. The context is fetched once at the start of compute() and then passed down as a parameter.

      Since:
      2025.07 - GeneratorContext Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(BATCH_SIZE) on creation (due to pre-allocation); O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-local; each thread has its own instance of the context obtained via context.get().
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • allContexts

      private static final Queue<CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext> allContexts
      A thread-safe collection of all active CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext instances.

      This is the key to solving the final flush problem. Each time a GeneratorContext is created for a new thread, it adds itself to this static, concurrent queue. When flushAllPendingBatches() is called, it can safely iterate over this queue to access every thread's context and flush any remaining partial batches. We use a ConcurrentLinkedQueue to ensure thread-safe additions and safe iteration without locking.

      Since:
      2025.10 - Final Flush Refactor
      See Also:
      • ConcurrentLinkedQueue
      • ConcurrentLinkedQueue()
      • Queue
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) amortized insertion time; O(n) iteration time for flushing.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to the use of ConcurrentLinkedQueue.
      Memory Management:
      Grows with the number of generator threads; each entry has a minimal footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • numClicks

      private static int numClicks
      The target length of the combinations to be generated.

      This is a static field shared by all tasks. It is initialized by the root task and defines the recursion depth for the generation process.

      Since:
      2025.06 - Fork Join Refactor
      See Also:
      • prefix
      • prefixLength
      • CombinationGeneratorTask(int, CombinationQueueArray, short[], int)
      • compute()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • queueArray

      private static CombinationQueueArray queueArray
      The array of queues used to distribute WorkBatch objects to monkeys.

      To balance the workload, generators randomly select a queue from this array when flushing a batch. This static field is initialized by the root task.

      Since:
      2025.06 - Fork Join Refactor
      See Also:
      • CombinationGeneratorTask(int, CombinationQueueArray, short[], int)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • maxFirstClickIndex

      private static int maxFirstClickIndex
      The maximum index allowed for the first click in any combination.

      This is a simple but effective pruning optimization. Since combinations are generated in lexicographical order, we know that the first initially true cell must be toggled by one of the clicks in the combination. This value is pre-calculated as the highest-indexed cell adjacent to the first true cell, effectively pruning any combinations that start with a click beyond this point.

      Since:
      2025.06 - First Click Optimization
      See Also:
      • compute()
      • Grid.findFirstTrueAdjacents(com.github.mrgarbagegamer.Grid.ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • prefix

      private short[] prefix
      The sequence of clicks made so far, forming the base for sub-tasks or final combinations.

      Each task is defined by its prefix. For an intermediate task, it will fork new tasks by appending a new click to this prefix. For a leaf task, it will generate final combinations by appending a final click. The short[] holding the prefix is obtained from a pre-allocated ArrayPool to prevent heap allocation. To prevent array resizing, we always allocate arrays of size numClicks, tracking the current length of the prefix with prefixLength.

      Since:
      2025.06 - Fork Join Refactor
      See Also:
      • recycleOwnResources(GeneratorContext)
      • ArrayPool
      • ArrayPool.get()
      • ArrayPool.put(short[])
      • CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext
      • CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.prefixArrayPool
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for adding clicks, O(prefixLength) for copying to subtasks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~numClicks * 2 bytes per task, using pooling to avoid allocations.

    • prefixLength

      private int prefixLength
      The current length of the prefix array.

      This value determines the task's position in the generation tree (root, intermediate, or leaf) and is used as an index for appending the next click, since prefix arrays are always sized to numClicks.

      Since:
      2025.06 - Fork Join Refactor
      See Also:
      • compute()
      • prefix
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 4 bytes as an int.

    • cachedAdjacencyState

      private long cachedAdjacencyState
      A bitmask representing the set of initially true cells toggled by the current prefix.

      This is a critical field for early-stage pruning. Instead of recomputing which true cells are affected by a prefix, each task inherits the state from its parent and updates it incrementally with a single OR operation. This state is then used in canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int) to determine if the current path is viable. A value of -1 indicates an uninitialized state, used only by the root task.

      The use of a long limits this check to puzzles with 64 or fewer initially true cells, but all puzzles simulated in this project fall under this limit.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Cached adjacency state introduction
      See Also:
      • skipConstraintsCheck
      • TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS
      • canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int)
      • ensureTrueCellMasks(short[])
      • init(short[], int, long, boolean, boolean)
      • Grid.areAdjacent(short, short)
      • Grid.findTrueCells()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(prefixLength) for initial computation, and O(1) for updates.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 8 bytes as a long.

    • prefixParity

      private boolean prefixParity
      A boolean indicating the parity of clicks affecting the first initially true cell in the prefix. true indicates an odd number of toggles, false indicates even.

      Like cachedAdjacencyState, this value is incrementally built in the generator threads to allow for O(1) checks in leaf tasks.

      Since:
      2025.10 - Prefix Parity Pre-computation
      See Also:
      • computeIntermediateSubtasksConstraintPath(GeneratorContext)
      • computeIntermediateSubtasksSkipPath(GeneratorContext)
      • init(short[], int, long, boolean, boolean)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for updates during task initialization, which avoids a previously O(prefixLength) operation in the leaf task path.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 1 byte as a boolean.

    • skipConstraintsCheck

      private boolean skipConstraintsCheck
      A flag indicating that this task and all its descendants are guaranteed to satisfy the constraints, allowing future checks to be skipped.

      This flag is set to true by canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int) when a prefix is found to toggle every initially true cell. Because additional clicks cannot "un-satisfy" this condition, all child tasks spawned from this point can inherit this flag and bypass the expensive constraint check. This enables a much faster, branch-free generation path in computeIntermediateSubtasksSkipPath(GeneratorContext).

      Since:
      2025.08 - Reduced Branching Refactor
      See Also:
      • cachedAdjacencyState
      • computeIntermediateSubtasks(GeneratorContext)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for checks and updates.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 1 byte as a boolean.

    • targetMask

      private static long targetMask
      A bitmask where each bit corresponds to an initially true cell, and all bits are set to 1.

      This mask represents the goal state for the canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int) check. A prefix is considered to have directly satisfied the constraints when its cachedAdjacencyState is equal to this targetMask. It is computed once by the root task and shared statically to avoid re-computation in the hot path.

      Since:
      2025.08 - Reduced Branching Refactor
      See Also:
      • SUFFIX_OR_MASKS
      • ensureTrueCellMasks(short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(trueCells.length) for initial computation, and O(1) for checks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 8 bytes as a long.

    • generatorPool

      private static volatile ForkJoinPool generatorPool
      The ForkJoinPool that executes the generator tasks.

      A static reference to the pool is maintained so that a TestClickCombination thread can signal a global shutdown by calling ForkJoinPool.shutdownNow() on it as soon as a solution is found. This is the primary mechanism for terminating the search.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Self-Cleanup and Cancellation Refactor
      See Also:
      • TestClickCombination.triggerGeneratorShutdown()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to the use of a static volatile field.
      Memory Management:
      Minimal memory footprint of 4 bytes as a reference.

    • FIRST_TRUE_ADJACENTS

      private static long[] FIRST_TRUE_ADJACENTS
      A bitmask representing the cells adjacent to the first initially true cell.

      This mask is used in a lightweight, leaf-level pruning check inside computeLeafCombinations(GeneratorContext). The check ensures that the first true cell is toggled an odd number of times, which is a requirement for a valid solution. It is pre-computed once by the root task's constructor.

      A long[2] array is used to represent the bitmask for all 109 cells, as a single long is insufficient. This allows for fast, cache-friendly bitwise operations at the cost of requiring two checks per cell in the leaf task loop.

      Since:
      2025.06 - Revamped Odd Adjacency Check
      See Also:
      • Grid.findAdjacents(short)
      • Grid.findFirstTrueCell(Grid.ValueFormat)
      • Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(Grid.findFirstTrueAdjacents().length) for initial computation, and O(1) for checks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~16 bytes as two longs.

    • ODD_CLICK_INDICES

      private static short[] ODD_CLICK_INDICES
      An array of pre-computed indices of all clicks with odd parity.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Pre-computed Parity Masks
      See Also:
      • Grid.findAdjacents(short)
      • Grid.findFirstTrueCell(Grid.ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(ODD_CLICK_INDICES.length) for iteration in leaf tasks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint proportional to the number of odd parity clicks.

    • EVEN_CLICK_INDICES

      private static short[] EVEN_CLICK_INDICES
      An array of pre-computed indices of all clicks with even parity.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Pre-computed Parity Masks
      See Also:
      • Grid.findAdjacents(short)
      • Grid.findFirstTrueCell(Grid.ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(EVEN_CLICK_INDICES.length) for iteration in leaf tasks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to immutability after initialization.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint proportional to the number of even parity clicks.

    • TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS

      private static long[] TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS
      An array of bitmasks where each mask represents which of the initially true cells a given click is adjacent to.

      This is a core data structure for pruning. TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS[i] is a long where the k-th bit is 1 if and only if clicking cell i toggles the k-th initially true cell.

      This allows for O(1) updates to the cachedAdjacencyState (e.g., state ^= TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS[click]) instead of expensive re-computation. It is initialized once per puzzle run by ensureTrueCellMasks(short[]). The use of a long limits this optimization to puzzles with 64 or fewer true cells.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Adjacency Checks
      See Also:
      • canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int)
      • Grid.findAdjacents(short, Grid.ValueFormat, Grid.ValueFormat)
      • Grid.findTrueCells(Grid.ValueFormat)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for adjacency checks, O(trueCells.length) for initial computation.
      Threading Model:
      Statically initialized once by ensureTrueCellMasks(short[])
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~8 * Grid.NUM_CELLS bytes as a long array.

    • SUFFIX_OR_MASKS

      private static long[] SUFFIX_OR_MASKS
      An array of pre-computed "suffix OR" bitmasks for O(1) constraint checking.

      This is a critical optimization for canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int). The mask at index i is the bitwise OR of all TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS from index i to the end of the grid.

      In effect, SUFFIX_OR_MASKS[i] represents every true cell that can possibly be toggled by any future click from index i onwards. This allows the constraint check to determine if a solution is still possible in a single O(1) bitwise operation, rather than iterating through all remaining clicks (though the use of a long limits this optimization to puzzles with 64 or fewer true cells). It is initialized once per puzzle run by ensureTrueCellMasks(short[]).

      Since:
      2025.07 - Suffix OR Masks Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for constraint checks, O(Grid.NUM_CELLS) for initial computation.
      Threading Model:
      Statically initialized once by ensureTrueCellMasks(short[]) and effectively final thereafter.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~8 * Grid.NUM_CELLS bytes as a long array.

    • CLICK_ADJACENCY_MATRIX

      private static final boolean[][] CLICK_ADJACENCY_MATRIX
      A static adjacency matrix used to initialize TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS.

      CLICK_ADJACENCY_MATRIX[i][j] is true if clicking cell i toggles cell j. This is a legacy structure used during the one-time computation of the bitmasks. It is less efficient than the bitmask-based checks in Grid but is retained for this setup step, since it pre-dates the bitmask optimizations.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Adjacency Checks
      See Also:
      • initClickAdjacencyMatrix()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for adjacency checks, O(109²) for initial computation.
      Threading Model:
      Statically initialized once at class load time.
      Memory Management:
      Fixed memory footprint of ~109² bytes as a boolean matrix.

  • Constructor Details

    • CombinationGeneratorTask

      public CombinationGeneratorTask(int numClicks, CombinationQueueArray queueArray, short[] trueCells, int maxFirstClickIndex)
      Constructs the root task for combination generation.

      This special constructor is called only once per puzzle run. It is responsible for initializing all static fields that are shared across all generator tasks, including numClicks, queueArray, and maxFirstClickIndex. It also performs critical one-time pre-computations, such as building the various bitmasks (targetMask, TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS, etc.) that are essential for performance.

      Parameters:
      numClicks - The target number of clicks per combination.
      queueArray - The array of queues for distributing work.
      trueCells - A short[] of the initially true cell indices.
      maxFirstClickIndex - The pre-calculated maximum index for the first click.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if parameters are invalid.
      Since:
      2025.06 - Fork Join Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(trueCells.length) for initialization, amortized over the entire generation process.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate unless pools are empty (except for the root task).

    • CombinationGeneratorTask

      protected CombinationGeneratorTask()
      A protected constructor for non-root tasks, used exclusively by the TaskPool.

      To prevent heap allocations, new tasks are never created directly. Instead, they are recycled from a TaskPool. This constructor is therefore made protected to enforce the pooling pattern. Task state is configured via the init(short[], int, long, boolean, boolean) method.

      Since:
      2025.07 - Task Pool Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) allocation.
      Threading Model:
      Isolated by the ForkJoinTask framework.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a CombinationGeneratorTask instance.

  • Method Details

    • setForkJoinPool

      public static void setForkJoinPool(ForkJoinPool pool)
      Sets the ForkJoinPool used to execute generator tasks.

      This method is used to set the static pool field, allowing monkeys to signal cancellation when they find a solution. The pool is best set once at the start of generation and left constant thereafter, as changing it mid-generation could lead to inconsistent behavior.

      Parameters:
      pool - the ForkJoinPool to use for executing generator tasks.
      Since:
      2025.07.22 - Self-Cleanup and Cancellation Refactor
      See Also:
      • TestClickCombination.triggerGeneratorShutdown()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) assignment.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to the use of a static volatile field, though this method should be called by one thread only.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • getForkJoinPool

      public static ForkJoinPool getForkJoinPool()
      Gets the ForkJoinPool used to execute generator tasks.

      This method provides access to the static pool field, allowing monkeys to signal cancellation when they find a solution.

      Returns:
      the ForkJoinPool used for executing generator tasks.
      Since:
      2025.07.22 - Self-Cleanup and Cancellation Refactor
      See Also:
      • TestClickCombination.triggerGeneratorShutdown()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) access time.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to the use of a static volatile field.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • computeAdjacencyMaskFast

      private static void computeAdjacencyMaskFast(short firstTrueCell)
      Computes and caches bitmasks and index arrays for parity checks.

      This is a one-time operation performed by the root task. It computes the FIRST_TRUE_ADJACENTS bitmask and then uses it to classify all 109 possible clicks into two groups: those with "odd" parity (they are adjacent to the first true cell) and those with "even" parity (they are not).

      The indices of these clicks are stored in the ODD_CLICK_INDICES and EVEN_CLICK_INDICES arrays. This pre-computation allows the leaf generation to iterate over a much smaller, pre-filtered set of valid final clicks, significantly improving performance.

      Parameters:
      firstTrueCell - The index of the first true cell.
      Since:
      2025.06 - Bitmasked Leaf Pruning Introduction
      See Also:
      • CombinationGeneratorTask(int, CombinationQueueArray, short[], int)
      • Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(Grid.NUM_CELLS) for initial computation.
      Threading Model:
      Single-threaded during root task initialization, no synchronization needed.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates fixed-size arrays for masks and indices.

    • compute

      protected void compute()
      The main computation method for the RecursiveAction.

      This method acts as a dispatcher, determining the task's role based on its prefixLength and delegating to the appropriate computation path:

      • Root Task (prefixLength == 0): Invokes computeRootSubtasks(GeneratorContext).
      • Leaf Task (prefixLength == numClicks - 1): Invokes computeLeafCombinations(GeneratorContext).
      • Intermediate Task (otherwise): Invokes computeIntermediateSubtasks(GeneratorContext).
      A finally block ensures that recycleOwnResources(GeneratorContext) is always called to return the task and its prefix array to their respective pools. The context is fetched once at the start to minimize ThreadLocal access overhead.

      Specified by:
      compute in class RecursiveAction
      Since:
      2025.06 - Work-stealing introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) context access, and O(1) dispatching.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; uses pooled resources.

    • computeRootSubtasks

      private void computeRootSubtasks(CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext ctx)
      Computes and forks the first level of subtasks from the root task.

      This method iterates through all valid first clicks, creating and forking a new subtask for each one. The range of iteration is limited by maxFirstClickIndex as a pruning optimization.

      The helpQuiesce() Workaround

      After forking all subtasks, this method calls ForkJoinTask.helpQuiesce(). This is a critical workaround for a surprising behavior in ForkJoinPool: ForkJoinPool.awaitQuiescence(long, java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit) does not wait for tasks forked by the task it was invoked on. If we didn't block here, the main thread would un-park prematurely, long before the search is complete.

      Using helpQuiesce() forces the root task (and by extension, the main thread that invoked it) to participate in work-stealing until all its descendant tasks are complete. It's a strange quirk, but this is the correct way to ensure the entire computation tree finishes before the program exits.

      Parameters:
      ctx - The thread-local CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.
      Since:
      2025.08 - Reduced Branching Refactor
      See Also:
      • recycleOwnResources(GeneratorContext)
      • StartYourMonkeys.main(String[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(N) where N is the number of valid first clicks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate unless pools are empty.

    • init

      public void init(short[] prefix, int prefixLength, long parentAdjacencyState, boolean skipConstraints, boolean prefixParity)
      Initializes a recycled task with a new prefix and state.

      This method is central to the object pooling strategy. Instead of creating a new task, we recycle an existing one from the TaskPool and re-initialize it with a new prefix and state. It assigns the given parameters and calls ForkJoinTask.reinitialize() to reset the ForkJoinTask state, making the task ready for re-submission.

      This method was intentionally designed as a single, monomorphic call site. Previous versions had multiple init overloads, which hindered JIT compiler optimizations. By consolidating them into one method, we improve performance and allow for greater JIT optimizations, at the small cost of the occasional unnecessary assignment.

      Parameters:
      prefix - The prefix short[] for the new task.
      prefixLength - The length of the prefix.
      parentAdjacencyState - The cachedAdjacencyState from the parent task.
      skipConstraints - A flag indicating if constraint checks can be skipped.
      prefixParity - The parity of the prefix affecting the first true cell.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Task Pool Introduction
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for assignments and reinitialization.
      Threading Model:
      Not thread-safe; must be called by only one thread at a time on a given task.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; uses pooled resources.

    • computeLeafCombinations

      private final void computeLeafCombinations(CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext ctx)
      Defines a work range for a leaf task and adds it to a WorkBatch.

      This is the final, non-recursive step in the generation process. In the refactored design, this method no longer generates individual combinations. Instead, it defines a WorkBatch.WorkItem that represents all valid final combinations for the current prefix. This dramatically reduces the generator's workload.

      Algorithm

      1. Pre-filtered Clicks: It selects the correct array of potential final clicks (ODD_CLICK_INDICES or EVEN_CLICK_INDICES) based on the pre-computed prefixParity.
      2. Binary Search: It performs a binary search on that array to find the first valid final click that can follow the current prefix in lexicographical order.
      3. WorkItem Batching: It then creates a single WorkBatch.WorkItem representing the prefix and the entire valid range of final clicks by calling WorkBatch.addWork(short[], int, boolean, int).
      If the current batch is full, it is flushed before the new work item is added. This bulk-processing approach dramatically reduces method call overhead and loop-related costs, making it significantly more efficient than an iterative approach.

      Parameters:
      ctx - The thread-local CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Splitting the Compute Method Into Paths
      See Also:
      • WorkBatch.addWork(short[], int, boolean, int)
      • CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.getOrCreateBatch()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(log N) binary search, where N is the number of valid final clicks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Algorithm Details:
      Uses binary search to find a starting index, then adds a single WorkItem to the batch representing the entire remaining range.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; uses pooled resources.

    • computeIntermediateSubtasks

      private void computeIntermediateSubtasks(CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext ctx)
      Dispatches to the appropriate intermediate subtask computation method based on the skipConstraintsCheck flag.

      This method serves as a simple, fast dispatcher for all intermediate tasks (i.e., non-root, non-leaf). Its sole purpose is to route control to one of two specialized computation paths, which is a critical optimization for the JIT compiler.

      Monomorphic Call Site Optimization

      A key performance goal is to avoid branching inside the hot loops where subtasks are forked. If a task's prefix is already known to satisfy the constraint check, all its descendants can skip that check. Instead of handling this with an if statement inside a single, large method, we use two separate methods:

      • computeIntermediateSubtasksConstraintPath(GeneratorContext): The "slow" path that includes the pruning check.
      • computeIntermediateSubtasksSkipPath(GeneratorContext): The "fast" path that omits the check entirely.
      This dispatcher creates a monomorphic call site for each path. The JIT compiler can more aggressively optimize these separate, branch-free methods than it could a single method containing a conditional check, leading to significant performance gains. It also simplifies the code for compute(), designating a clear input method for intermediate tasks without an additional conditional.

      Parameters:
      ctx - The thread-local CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.
      Since:
      2025.08 - Reduced Branching Refactor
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for dispatching.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

    • computeIntermediateSubtasksSkipPath

      private void computeIntermediateSubtasksSkipPath(CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext ctx)
      Forks subtasks for an intermediate task, skipping the constraint check.

      This is the "fast path" for intermediate tasks. It is invoked when the current task's prefix has already been proven to satisfy the constraint check, meaning all its descendants are also guaranteed to be on a valid path.

      This method contains a highly optimized, branch-free loop. It iterates through all possible next clicks, gets a recycled task and prefix from the pools, initializes them, and forks them for execution. Because no pruning checks are needed, the loop body is minimal and extremely friendly to JIT compiler optimizations. A small safeguard exists to allocate a new array if the ArrayPool is exhausted, though this is not expected in normal operation and could be removed for a slight performance gain at the cost of robustness.

      Parameters:
      ctx - The thread-local CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext containing the resource pools.
      Since:
      2025.08 - Specialized Subtask Paths
      See Also:
      • skipConstraintsCheck
      • computeIntermediateSubtasksConstraintPath(GeneratorContext)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(Grid.NUM_CELLS - numClicks + prefixLength - prefix[prefixLength - 1] + 1) for iterating over possible next clicks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate unless pools are empty; uses pooled resources.

    • computeIntermediateSubtasksConstraintPath

      private void computeIntermediateSubtasksConstraintPath(CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext ctx)
      Forks subtasks for an intermediate task, performing a constraint check to prune invalid branches.

      This is the "slow path" for intermediate tasks. It is invoked when the current task's prefix has not yet been proven to satisfy all constraints. Before forking subtasks, it performs a critical pruning step by calling canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int). If that check determines that no possible descendant of this task can form a valid solution, the entire branch is pruned, saving a massive amount of wasted computation.

      If the path is viable, the method proceeds to fork subtasks in a tight loop. For each new subtask, it incrementally updates the cachedAdjacencyState with a single OR operation and passes it down. It also propagates the skipConstraintsCheck flag, which may have been set to true inside canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints, allowing all subsequent children to use the "fast path" from this point forward. To keep the loop free of conditionals, the adjacency state is calculated even if the check passes and sets the skip flag, which is a minor trade-off for a more JIT-friendly method body. A similar safeguard for the ArrayPool also exists here.

      Parameters:
      ctx - The thread-local CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext containing the resource pools.
      Since:
      2025.08 - Specialized Subtask Paths
      See Also:
      • computeIntermediateSubtasksSkipPath(GeneratorContext)
      • ensureTrueCellMasks(short[])
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for the early constraint check, O(Grid.NUM_CELLS - numClicks + prefixLength - prefix[prefixLength - 1] + 1) for iterating over possible next clicks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate unless pools are empty; uses pooled resources.

    • canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints

      private boolean canPotentiallySatisfyConstraints(int startIdx)
      Checks if the current prefix and its descendants can potentially form a valid solution.

      This is the primary pruning mechanism in the generation tree. A valid solution must toggle every initially true cell. This method uses bitmasks to determine if it's possible for the current prefix, combined with any future clicks, to meet this condition. If not, the entire branch of the search tree is abandoned, saving immense computation.

      Algorithm

      The check is performed in two stages, both using O(1) bitwise operations:

      1. Direct Check: It computes the needed bits by XORing the cachedAdjacencyState (which true cells are toggled by the current prefix) with the targetMask (all true cells). If needed is zero, the constraint is already met. As an optimization, it sets skipConstraintsCheck to true, allowing all descendants to use a faster, check-free generation path.
      2. Potential Check: If the constraint is not yet met, it checks if it's still possible to meet it. It uses a pre-computed SUFFIX_OR_MASKS bitmask, which represents all the true cells that could be toggled by any of the remaining available clicks. If the bitwise AND of this "available" mask and the needed mask equals the needed mask, it means a solution is still possible, and the path is not pruned.
      This allows the method to prune with certainty without needing to iterate through future clicks.

      Parameters:
      startIdx - The starting index for the next click, used to look up the correct suffix mask.
      Returns:
      true if this path is still viable, false if it should be pruned.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Splitting the Compute Method Into Paths
      See Also:
      • TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS
      • Grid.findAdjacents(short, Grid.ValueFormat, Grid.ValueFormat)
      • Grid.findTrueCells(Grid.ValueFormat)
      • Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) due to the use of pre-computed bitmasks.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate; uses pre-computed static bitmasks.

    • initClickAdjacencyMatrix

      private static boolean[][] initClickAdjacencyMatrix()
      Initializes the static CLICK_ADJACENCY_MATRIX upon class loading.

      This method performs a one-time computation, iterating through every cell in the grid and using Grid.findAdjacents(short, Grid.ValueFormat) to populate the adjacency matrix. It is called only once when the class is loaded.

      Returns:
      A boolean[Grid.NUM_CELLS][Grid.NUM_CELLS matrix representing cell adjacencies.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Adjacency Checks
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(Grid.NUM_CELLS²), due to nested iteration over cells and their adjacents.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe as it is called once during class loading and creates an immutable result.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates a fixed-size adjacency matrix of Grid.NUM_CELLS² bytes.

    • ensureTrueCellMasks

      private static void ensureTrueCellMasks(short[] trueCells)
      Initializes the static TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS and SUFFIX_OR_MASKS for a given puzzle.

      This method performs the one-time, expensive pre-computation of the bitmasks used for pruning. It is called by the root task and uses double-checked locking to ensure the computation happens only once, even if multiple threads somehow invoke it.

      Algorithm

      The process has two main steps:

      1. Adjacency Masks: It iterates through every possible click cell (0 to 109). For each one, it builds a bitmask representing which of the puzzle's trueCells it is adjacent to, using the CLICK_ADJACENCY_MATRIX. The results are stored in TRUE_CELL_ADJACENCY_MASKS.
      2. Suffix OR Masks: It then iterates backwards over the newly created adjacency masks to compute the SUFFIX_OR_MASKS. Each entry SUFFIX_OR_MASKS[i] is the cumulative bitwise OR of all adjacency masks from i to the end.
      This pre-computation is what enables subsequent O(1) constraint checks.

      Parameters:
      trueCells - The short[] of initially true cell indices for the current puzzle.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Bitmasked Adjacency Checks
      See Also:
      • Grid.ValueFormat.Bitmask
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to a synchronized block with double-checked locking.
      Memory Management:
      Allocates two fixed-size arrays of ~109 * 8 bytes each.

    • flushBatchFast

      private final boolean flushBatchFast(WorkBatch batch)
      Flushes a batch of combinations to an available queue, employing a backoff strategy if all queues are temporarily full.

      This method is a critical component of the producer-consumer architecture, ensuring that generated combinations are efficiently transferred to monkeys for validation. It implements a robust mechanism to handle queue contention and backpressure without dropping any combinations.

      Algorithm

      The method attempts to add the WorkBatch to a randomly selected queue from the queueArray. To minimize contention, it tries each queue once in a round-robin fashion. If all queues are full, the thread sleeps briefly (0.5ms) to avoid busy-waiting, then retries. This loop continues indefinitely until the batch is successfully enqueued or the thread is interrupted. This "never drop a combination" rule is fundamental to the solver's correctness.

      Future Optimizations

      The current backpressure mechanism (sleeping) is simple but can be improved. Potential enhancements include:

      • Using ForkJoinPool.ManagedBlocker to allow the pool to compensate for blocked generator threads by spinning up other workers.
      • Implementing a more sophisticated blocking strategy (e.g., using a dedicated signal from consumers) to wake generator threads only when queue space is available, avoiding unnecessary sleeps. This could, however, increase complexity, potentially requiring a shift to a different queue structure.

      Parameters:
      batch - The WorkBatch containing generated combinations to be flushed.
      Returns:
      true if the batch was successfully flushed, false if the thread was interrupted before flushing could complete.
      Since:
      2025.06.08 - Fork Join Refactor
      See Also:
      • CombinationQueue.add(WorkBatch)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(numQueues) per attempt, with a brief sleep if all queues are full.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to local queue access and atomic operations within CombinationQueue.add(WorkBatch).

    • recycleOwnResources

      private void recycleOwnResources(CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext ctx)
      Recycles the prefix and the CombinationGeneratorTask instance back to their respective pools within the provided CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext.

      This method is invoked by each CombinationGeneratorTask upon completion of its compute() method. It is a fundamental part of the object pooling strategy, preventing excessive heap allocations and subsequent garbage collection overhead during the high-volume generation process.

      By returning objects to pools instead of allowing them to be garbage collected, the solver maintains a low memory footprint and consistent performance, avoiding "stop-the-world" pauses.

      Parameters:
      ctx - The thread-local CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext containing the ArrayPool and TaskPool.
      Since:
      2025.07 - Task Self-Recycling
      See Also:
      • context
      • ArrayPool
      • ArrayPool.put(short[])
      • TaskPool
      • TaskPool.put(CombinationGeneratorTask)
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(1) for returning resources.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to ForkJoinTask isolation and thread-local GeneratorContext.

    • flushAllPendingBatches

      public static void flushAllPendingBatches()
      Flushes any remaining pending batches from a single thread's CombinationGeneratorTask.GeneratorContext to the CombinationQueueArray.

      This method is called once by the main thread after all generator tasks have completed. Its purpose is to ensure that any partially filled WorkBatches, which might not have reached WorkBatch.BATCH_SIZE and thus weren't flushed during normal operation, are nonetheless processed.

      It iterates through all known GeneratorContext instances (tracked in allContexts), checks if they have a non-empty current batch, and flushes it using a blocking strategy to guarantee delivery. It also logs the start and completion of the flush process for transparency.

      Since:
      2025.06 - Flush Batches when Full (or on Completion)
      See Also:
      • context
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(numQueues) for the flushing operation.
      Threading Model:
      Synchronized to prevent concurrent invocations.
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate, apart from some logging overhead.

    • flushBatchBlocking

      private static void flushBatchBlocking(WorkBatch batch)
      Flushes a WorkBatch to the CombinationQueueArray, blocking if necessary until successful.

      This method is similar to flushBatchFast(WorkBatch), but it employs a blocking strategy to ensure that the batch is eventually flushed, even if all queues are temporarily full. It is used during the final flush of pending batches to guarantee that no combinations are lost.

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the thread is interrupted while sleeping.
      Since:
      2025.10 - Guaranteed Flush for Final Batches
      See Also:
      • flushAllPendingBatches()
      Performance Characteristics:
      O(numQueues) per attempt, with a brief sleep if all queues are full.
      Threading Model:
      Thread-safe due to local queue access and atomic operations within CombinationQueue.add(WorkBatch).
      Memory Management:
      Does not allocate.

Performance-optimized brute-force solver for hexagonal grid Lights Out puzzles
